sections.paragraph:
[“a demonstrative particle originally of place, in that place, there, kindred with זֶה; Arab. إِذْ, be-hold!” called in Man.]; subst. time (from the root אָזָה, comp. עָר), hence in accusat. at that time, then, specially-

(1) damals, then, of past time; Arabic إِذ then, and إِذْ tunc, then, thereon; Germ. da; in apodosis, behold! fiehe da fo; Chald. אֱדַיִן; Gen 12:6 Jos 10:12 14:11 . Followed by a preterite, 1Ki 8:12 2Ch 6:1 8:12 , 17 2Ch 8:17and a future, used for a preterite, Jos. l. cit.; Exo 15:1 Deu 4:41. Comp. Lehrg. p. 773.

(2) then, after that, of future time. Construed with a fut. which retains its own power; Psa 96:12, אָז יְרַנְּנוּ “then shall they rejoice;” Zep 3:9 Job 3:13 sometimes also with a preterite in the signification of the future, where a future precedes, Jud. 5:11 Exo 15:15.

(3) then, after that, for therefore, because of that, Jer 22:15 Psa 40:8.
